effect_excerpt: Table 1 shows summary statistics for ineligible and eligible individuals before and after DACA became available. The sample consists of Mexican-born Hispanic non-citizen population aged 15 - 35. Difference in terms employment were already clear from the above figure 1. There are slightly more women among eligible individuals than ineligible individuals. Eligible individuals are slightly younger than ineligible individuals and have arrived to the USA at a significantly younger age. These individuals also have more years of education. Considering the significant age difference observed, differences in marital status between two groups of individuals are understandable. Eligible individuals appear to have slightly more family members. Significantly large differences are observed in the English language capabilities among the two groups of individuals. Many more eligible individuals are observed to speak English than ineligible individuals. Overall, the two groups of individuals are observed to have similar state level characteristics. More significant difference among state level characteristics is observed in in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants after DACA become available. Many more states where eligible individuals reside appear to provide in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants especially after DACA became available.
